name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash offers payday loans up to $425, lines of credit up to $4,000, and title loans up to $4,000 with in-store and online application options at Memphis locations.
description_long:
Speedy Cash operates as a storefront lender providing short-term emergency lending solutions across multiple Memphis, Tennessee locations. The company has been serving customers through physical retail stores offering rapid funding for unexpected expenses and cash flow gaps. Their Memphis Summer Avenue location operates extended hours (9am-7pm most weekdays, 9am-4pm Saturday, closed Sunday) to accommodate working customers seeking quick access to capital. Speedy Cash positions itself as a neighborhood lender focused on accessibility and customer service.

The company offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans (up to $425 with repayment due on next payday), Lines of Credit (up to $4,000 with flexible draw-down and repayment over time), and Title Loans (up to $4,000 secured by vehicle collateral). Beyond lending, Speedy Cash provides ancillary money services including money orders, wire transfers, check cashing, and Green Dot Visa debit cards. Customers can apply online or in-store, with in-store visits providing opportunity for live questions and document review with staff members.

Key distinguishing features include transparent pricing disclosure before loan agreement signing, flexible credit limits that replenish as balances are paid down (for lines of credit), and vehicle appraisal services conducted in-store for title loans. The company emphasizes customer education, requiring applicants to understand loan terms, costs, and repayment expectations before committing. Multiple Memphis locations (8+ stores listed) provide geographic convenience, and dual-language customer service (English 1-888-333-1360, Spanish 1-855-734-0111) extends accessibility.

However, Speedy Cash operates in the high-cost lending category where APRs and fees are not disclosed on the website, making true cost comparison impossible before application. The payday loan maximum of $425 is substantially below personal loan thresholds, positioning this as emergency-only financing. These products carry inherent risks of debt cycles and should only be used when no alternatives exist. The company does not appear to offer credit counseling or debt management education despite serving financially vulnerable populations.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding available through in-store application process
Flexible Line of Credit product (up to $4,000) with replenishing available balance as you pay down principal
Transparent pricing model—borrowers know exact cost before signing loan documents
Extended operating hours (until 7pm most weekdays, 9am Saturday) accommodating working customers
In-store vehicle appraisal for title loans eliminates separate valuation costs
Dual-language customer service (English and Spanish) with dedicated phone lines
Additional money services (check cashing, wire transfers, money orders) at same location for convenience
cons: APR, fees, and total cost of borrowing not disclosed on website—must apply to learn true lending cost
Payday loan maximum of $425 is extremely small and only suitable for minor emergencies
Title loan collateral puts vehicle at risk of repossession if repayment defaults occur
No indication of credit counseling, financial education, or debt management resources offered
High-cost lending structure creates significant risk of debt cycles and repeat borrowing
best_for: Employed individuals with unexpected small expenses ($100-$425) due before next paycheck
Vehicle owners needing larger emergency cash ($1,000-$4,000) who can safely pledge title as collateral
Customers requiring flexible borrowing through lines of credit for variable or ongoing expenses
Spanish-speaking borrowers needing accessible customer service and bilingual support
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is designed for employed individuals with clear income who need emergency cash ($100-$4,000) within days and cannot access traditional bank loans or credit. The main caveat is that APR and total lending costs are not disclosed online, requiring in-person application to understand true expense; these high-cost loans should only be used as last resort when no alternatives (credit cards, family loans, employer advances, credit union PALs) exist, as they carry significant debt cycle risk.
services: Title loans up to $4,000 (vehicle collateral required)
In-store vehicle appraisal for title loan qualification
In-person loan application and document review
Green Dot Visa debit card issuance
Loan cost transparency disclosure before agreement signing
